findings.title: Configuration is valid
findings.observed: fixture config parses, but live readiness failures are represented
findings.recommendation: Fix fail findings before migration or topology cutover.

findings.title: Execution candidate is op-geth
findings.recommendation: Candidate migration endpoints should be op-reth/reth. op-geth and op-program support ends May 31, 2026.

findings.title: Execution candidate lags reference
findings.recommendation: Investigate candidate sync health before migration cutover; keep op-reth running alongside the existing node until heads converge.

findings.title: Execution block comparison diverged
findings.recommendation: Do not cut over traffic until block hash, state root, transaction root, and receipt root comparisons match across the sampled window.

findings.title: Execution RPC sample mismatch
findings.observed: eth_getBlockByHash returned different output for the sampled common head
findings.recommendation: Treat RPC output mismatch as a cutover blocker until the candidate behavior is understood.

findings.title: op-node reports down
findings.recommendation: Restore source-node health before allowing light or sequencer op-nodes to depend on it.

findings.title: Follower RPC head lags source
findings.recommendation: Check follow-source connectivity and source-node availability before using this follower for production traffic.

findings.title: Deriver proxyd RPC is unreachable
findings.recommendation: Restore proxyd routing before using this endpoint as a production dependency for derivation or edge traffic.

findings.title: Interop fixture scope
findings.observed: basic dependency readiness only; no cross-chain message validation is implied
findings.recommendation: This fail fixture still represents endpoint readiness only, not full interop protocol validation.

findings.title: Interop dependency chain ID mismatch
findings.recommendation: Point every dependency RPC at the configured chain before relying on dependency-set readiness checks.
